findings.evidence: `BaseChange.map` is just `Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map (BialgHom.id K K) f` at lines 156-158; lines 176-192 prove identity/composition by tensor induction. Mathlib has only `Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map`, `map_tmul`, `map_toCoalgHom`, and `map_toAlgHom` at `Mathlib/RingTheory/Bialgebra/TensorProduct.lean:72-89`, while `Algebra.TensorProduct` already has the general `map_id`/`map_comp` pattern at `Mathlib/RingTheory/TensorProduct/Maps.lean:490-495`.
findings.fix: Add general lemmas such as `Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map_id` and `Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map_comp` for arbitrary tensor-product bialgebra maps, then derive these base-change special cases from them.
findings.issue: `map_id` and `map_comp` prove functoriality only for `BaseChange.map`, duplicating a general tensor-product fact for bialgebra homs.

summary: The base-change object and inclusion sit at the roadmap level, but the functoriality lemmas are proved only for the scalar-extension specialization even though the same statements belong first to `Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map`.
